headline_summary: Iran-related hostilities and threats around the Strait of Hormuz are disrupting maritime traffic and raising energy supply risk.
Commercial shipping has reportedly reduced transits and anchored vessels near the Strait amid projectile/drone incidents and war-risk repricing.
Israel-Hezbollah escalation in Lebanon is widening the conflict footprint with evacuation orders and strikes reported.
U.S. Treasury expanded Iran-related sanctions targeting shadow fleet vessels and networks tied to illicit petroleum sales and weapons programs.
risk_drivers: Effective closure / severe disruption risk at Strait of Hormuz (critical oil/LNG chokepoint)
Kinetic incidents impacting commercial shipping (projectiles/drones; casualties reported)
Broader regional escalation (Israel-Hezbollah hostilities in Lebanon)
Policy response and sanctions tightening affecting Iranian oil flows and shipping networks

Energy: Higher upside risk to Brent/WTI and European gas due to Hormuz disruption and tanker/LNG bottlenecks.
Wider Middle East crude differentials and higher freight/insurance premia; rerouting constraints (e.g., Red Sea/Yanbu alternatives).
FX: EM FX (especially high current-account deficit importers) faces renewed pressure from energy-import shock and risk-off positioning.
GCC currencies (pegs) stable mechanically but local liquidity/market confidence can weaken as conflict persists.
Rates: Oil-driven inflation impulse raises risk of higher front-end rates / delayed easing expectations.
Credit spreads can widen for shipping, airlines, and energy-intensive sectors; war-risk insurance costs feed into financing conditions.
Equities: Risk-off pressure on global equities; outperformance bias to upstream energy/defense; underperformance in transport/logistics.
Regional equity volatility elevated (GCC, Israel) amid escalation headlines and operational disruptions.

sources.title: Tanker heads to UAE port via Strait of Hormuz to load oil, sources say
Three tankers damaged in Gulf and one seafarer killed as US-Iran conflict escalates
Global oil and gas prices soar as Iran crisis disrupts shipping, production
Saudi Arabia tries to divert oil to Red Sea amid Iran war, faces reluctant shippers
US expands sanctions on Iran over oil sales, weapons programs
Israel orders evacuation of swathe of south Lebanon
IRGC says Iran in ‘complete control’ of Strait of Hormuz amid Trump threats

events.title: Iranian missiles hit central Israel (Tel Aviv district impacts reported)
events.implication: Direct Iran-Israel kinetic exchange increases escalation risk and broadens risk-off impulse.

events.title: Commercial shipping disrupted as Iran says navigation through Strait of Hormuz halted; multiple tanker incidents reported
events.implication: Higher freight/insurance costs and severe energy supply-chain disruption risk; supports oil_up_risk.

events.title: Rare tanker transit to load Murban crude via Strait of Hormuz amid shipping disruption (Pola to Jebel Dhanna)
events.implication: Suggests partial/episodic navigation but under elevated operational risk; volatility in flows persists.

events.title: Energy prices surge as Hormuz disruption persists; Iraq output cuts and attacks on energy/logistics sites reported
events.implication: Energy and shipping shock raises inflation risks and supports risk_off_flows; spillovers to gas and freight.

events.title: Saudi Aramco attempts to reroute crude exports to Yanbu to bypass Hormuz; pipeline/Red Sea constraints highlighted
events.implication: Rerouting alleviates some supply risk but raises freight costs and shifts risk to Red Sea routes.

events.title: Israel orders evacuation of swathe of southern Lebanon as hostilities with Hezbollah intensify; strike in Baalbek reported
events.implication: Escalation in Lebanon increases regional conflict premium and adds to risk-off dynamics.

events.title: U.S. expands Iran-related sanctions targeting shadow fleet vessels and networks enabling illicit petroleum sales and weapons programs
events.implication: Sanctions tightening can constrain marginal Iranian oil/logistics capacity and elevate compliance/insurance risk.

events.title: IRGC claims control of Strait of Hormuz; U.S. discusses potential naval escorts for tankers (as reported)
events.implication: Heightened risk of incidents/miscalculation around escort operations; supports oil_up_risk and risk_off_flows.

links.from: Commercial shipping disrupted as Iran says navigation through Strait of Hormuz halted; multiple tanker incidents reported
links.why: Disruption at the main Gulf chokepoint increases risk of supply shortfall and raises freight/insurance costs.

links.from: Energy prices surge as Hormuz disruption persists; Iraq output cuts and attacks on energy/logistics sites reported
links.why: Energy shock increases inflation uncertainty and downside growth risk, prompting defensive positioning.

links.from: U.S. expands Iran-related sanctions targeting shadow fleet vessels and networks enabling illicit petroleum sales and weapons programs
links.why: Sanctions and compliance risk can widen spreads for shipping/energy logistics and tighten financing conditions.

links.from: Iranian missiles hit central Israel (Tel Aviv district impacts reported)
links.why: Direct state-on-state strikes raise tail-risk and increase demand for safe havens.

links.from: Saudi Aramco attempts to reroute crude exports to Yanbu to bypass Hormuz; pipeline/Red Sea constraints highlighted
links.why: Rerouting may relieve some constraints but capacity limits and added route risks keep upside risk elevated.
